qa_pairs.question: Why is it recommended to create model checkpoints during TensorFlow training?
qa_pairs.answer: Creating model checkpoints is recommended to break long-running jobs into multiple shorter jobs, which can help them get allocated on the cluster more quickly. It also serves as a crucial way to prevent losing training progress due to unexpected code errors or node failures.

qa_pairs.question: How can you create a model checkpoint when training with Keras?
qa_pairs.answer: When training with Keras, you can create a checkpoint by utilizing the `callbacks` parameter of the `model.fit()` method. For example, you can use `tf.keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint` and specify `save_freq` (e.g., to "epoch") to save a snapshot of your model at the end of each training epoch. It is important to ensure that the specified `filepath` for the checkpoint already exists.
